questions.stem: An armature-controlled DC motor and its speed-torque characteristics is shown in Fig. 1(a) and Fig. 1(b). Parameters: Ra (armature resistance), Jm = 5 kg-m², Bm = 2 N-m s/rad, JL = 700 kg-m², BL = 800 N-m s/rad, gear ratio N1/N2 = 100/1000 = 1/10. Speed-torque curve: at ea = 100 V, stall torque = 500 N-m, no-load speed = 50 rad/s. Find the transfer function θL(s)/Ea(s).
common_errors: Forgetting to refer load inertia and friction through gear ratio squared to motor shaft
Misreading Km and Kb from speed-torque curve (slope vs intercept)
Not accounting for Ra in the electrical equation when Ra is unlabelled but implied
questions.solution_method: From speed-torque characteristic extract motor parameters: Km = T_stall/ea = 500/100 = 5 N-m/A (or V-s/rad for Kb by equality). Refer load J and B through gear ratio n=N1/N2=0.1 to motor shaft: Jeq = Jm + n²JL, Beq = Bm + n²BL. Write armature KVL and motor torque equations, Laplace transform, then apply gear ratio θL = n·θm to get θL(s)/Ea(s) = n·Km / [s((Ra(Jeq·s + Beq)) + Km·Kb)].

questions.stem: The schematic diagram of a servo motor is shown in Fig. 2(a). Draw the block diagram and determine the transfer function θL(s)/θR(s). Given: Ks = 30 V/rad, KA = 20 V/V, JL = 1 kg-m², BL = 4 Nm/rad/s, motor J and B negligible, linearised torque-speed curve (Fig. 2b): stall torque = 2 Nm, no-load speed = 3000 rpm with 115 V 50 Hz control winding. Gear ratios: θL/θm = 1/2, θm/θs = 1/1.
common_errors: Not converting no-load speed from rpm to rad/s before computing motor slope Bm
Forgetting to refer load inertia/friction through gear ratio squared
Incorrect feedback sign or missing Ks in the block diagram
Confusing θm/θs gear ratio with θL/θm gear ratio in the chain
questions.solution_method: Extract AC servomotor parameters from torque-speed curve: no-load speed ω0 = 3000 rpm = 100π rad/s, stall torque T0 = 2 Nm; motor slope (viscous drag) Bm_motor = T0/ω0 = 2/(100π), motor gain Km = T0/Vc = 2/115 (if referred to control voltage, but typically just slope suffices). With n = θL/θs = (1/2)·(1/1) = 1/2, refer load: Jeq = JL·n², Beq = BL·n². Motor TF (position): Gm(s) = Km/[s(Jeqs + Beq + Bm_motor_equivalent)]. Closed-loop TF θL(s)/θR(s) = Ks·KA·Gm(s)·(1/2) / [1 + Ks·KA·Gm(s)·(1/2)].

notes: Two-question tutorial on control components (Module 3). Both questions involve DC/AC servomotor TF derivation with gear trains. Q1 uses armature-controlled DC motor with numerical graph data; Q2 uses AC servomotor with synchro error detector, amplifier, and gear train in a closed-loop position control system. Figures (circuit diagram, torque-speed curves, servo schematic) are image-based — parameter values extracted from figure annotations.
